headline: Fourteen Managers, One Crown: The MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball 2023 Campaign Begins
subheadline: As the Albuquerque Bombers defend their throne and twelve hungry franchises circle, the March 22nd draft promises to reshape the balance of power in ways this league has never seen.
body:
**I. The Gathering Storm**

The cactus flowers are blooming across Arizona, and the familiar crack of bat meeting ball echoes through the spring training complexes of the Grapefruit and Cactus Leagues. Pitchers are stretching out their arms in the morning sun. Position players are finding their timing again. And in living rooms and home offices across fourteen different time zones, the managers of MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball are doing what they have done every March for years now: studying, scheming, and preparing for war.

This is the moment before the moment. The calm before the chaos of draft night. Mock drafts have been run. Sleeper lists have been compiled. Arguments about whether to punt saves or chase counting stats have been rehearsed in group chats that have grown conspicuously quiet as March 22nd approaches. Everyone is watching everyone else, wondering who has found the edge, who has lost their fastball, and who might be the surprise package that emerges from the middle of the pack to claim glory.

And yet, for all the uncertainty that swirls around this league, one truth remains constant: the **Albuquerque Bombers** are the hunted. Champions of 2022. A franchise that finished second in 2021 before ascending to the throne last season. They are the standard by which all others will be measured, and there is no lonelier position in fantasy baseball than defending a title. The **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** finished agonizingly close, runners-up who surely spent the offseason replaying every waiver wire decision. The **Hiroshima Radiation** claimed third, a podium finish that suggests they are building something sustainable. The **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** have been knocking on the door for two consecutive seasons. And then there is **BaseballPro77**, a franchise that has finished fourth and fifth the last two years—close enough to taste contention, far enough to feel the sting of near-misses.

The draft order has been set. The board is arranged. On March 22nd, fourteen managers will make their declarations, and by the time the final pick is announced, the 2023 season will have already begun to take shape. This is not merely a draft—it is the first battle in a six-month campaign that will test friendship, patience, and the ability to function on three hours of sleep during trade deadline week.

**II. The Draft Order: Fortune's Wheel**

In snake draft formats, draft position is destiny—or at least the first chapter of it. The top tier of this draft belongs to **BaseballPro77** at pick one, **Sugar Land Skeeters** at two, **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** at three, and **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** at four. These managers will select from the truly elite—the Ronald Acuña Jrs., the Trea Turners, the five-category monsters who anchor championship rosters. But they will also wait the longest between picks, watching thirty-nine selections go by before their second pick arrives in the early second round. The pressure to nail that first selection is immense; a reach here echoes throughout the roster.

The sweet spot of this draft may well be picks five through eight: **Portage County Heartbreakers**, **Canberra Wombats**, **Albuquerque Bombers**, and **Central Wisconsin Curd Fryers**. These managers will sacrifice the very top tier of talent but gain advantageous snake-back positioning. When the turn comes in rounds two and three, they will make consecutive selections that can define their roster construction. The **Bombers**, drafting seventh as defending champions, find themselves in this favorable middle ground—a small mercy given the target on their back.

The back of the draft—picks nine through fourteen—is where creativity becomes necessity. **Pablo Sanchez's Drug Dealer**, **Hung Jung**, **Benchwarmers**, **Buttville Skidmarks**, **Riverside Roughnecks**, and **Hiroshima Radiation** will watch the elite tier evaporate before their eyes, but they will also enjoy the fastest turnaround between picks. History in this league has shown that late-drafting managers who identify value in the mid-rounds can absolutely compete. But it requires precision. It requires homework. And it requires the discipline to avoid chasing the players you wanted but cannot have.

**III. The Field: Fourteen Teams, Fourteen Stories**

**BaseballPro77** (Pick 1): The first overall selection is a crown and a burden. This franchise has finished fourth and fifth in consecutive seasons—respectable, certainly, but not the results of a true contender. The first pick offers **BaseballPro77** the opportunity to finally acquire the cornerstone piece that has eluded them. Expect a safe, high-floor selection here rather than a boom-or-bust gamble. If this franchise can pair elite first-round talent with the steady roster construction they have shown in recent years, a leap into the top three is possible. But history suggests they may once again fall just short of the prize. *Projected finish: 4th*

**Sugar Land Skeeters** (Pick 2): A twelfth-place finish in 2022 was not the result this franchise had in mind. The second overall pick represents a chance at redemption—a lottery ticket that actually hit. The **Skeeters** have the opportunity to select a generational talent and completely reshape their roster identity. The question is whether last year's collapse was a fluke or a symptom of deeper roster construction issues. One elite player does not make a champion, but it can certainly be the foundation of one. This is a franchise at a crossroads. *Projected finish: 8th*

**Abbotsford-Colby Twins** (Pick 3): Consistency thy name is **Twins**. A third-place finish in 2021 followed by fourth in 2022 demonstrates that this franchise understands how to build a competitive roster. The third pick gives them access to the elite tier, and with their track record of steady management, they should maximize the value of this draft position. This is a franchise that does not beat itself—they make the playoffs, they compete, and they trust their process. The only question is whether they can take the final step from perennial contender to champion. This may be their year. *Projected finish: 2nd*

**Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** (Pick 4): Runners-up in 2022. So close they could taste the championship, yet ultimately left empty-handed. The fourth pick is strong but not quite elite—much like their season last year. The **Wonderdogs** have proven they can compete at the highest level; now they must prove they can finish. There is a psychological element to losing in the championship round that can linger into the following season. Will they come back hungrier, or will the disappointment weigh on their decisions? This franchise has the talent evaluation skills to contend again. The question is whether they have the resolve. *Projected finish: 3rd*

**Portage County Heartbreakers** (Pick 5): A new name on the scene, or at least one without recent historical data to analyze. The fifth pick places them in an interesting position—just outside the elite tier but with excellent snake-back value. Mystery franchises are the hardest to project; they could be a sleeping giant or a pretender who fades by June. The name suggests drama, and this league has never been short on that. Watch this roster closely in the early weeks to understand what kind of manager is at the helm. *Projected finish: 10th*

**Canberra Wombats** (Pick 6): A sixth-place finish in 2022 established the **Wombats** as a respectable middle-tier franchise. The sixth pick maintains that status—solid but not spectacular. This is a team that needs to find an edge somewhere, whether through aggressive waiver wire work or a draft-day steal that shifts their ceiling. There is nothing wrong with being a solid citizen of the league, but solid citizens rarely raise trophies. The **Wombats** need to decide if they want to be comfortable or if they want to contend. *Projected finish: 6th*

**Albuquerque Bombers** (Pick 7): The defending champions draft from the seventh position, and while that is not ideal, it is far from disastrous. The **Bombers** won it all in 2022 after finishing second in 2021—a two-year run that establishes them as the league's premier franchise. They have proven they know how to build a roster, manage it through the grind of a long season, and peak at the right moment. The seventh pick offers solid value with favorable snake-back positioning. Do not expect this franchise to fade quietly. They have the institutional knowledge that comes with winning, and that is worth more than draft position. *Projected finish: 1st*

**Central Wisconsin Curd Fryers** (Pick 8): The eighth pick places the **Curd Fryers** at the end of the favorable middle tier. Without recent finish data to analyze, this is another franchise that operates in the shadows. The name suggests a Midwestern sensibility—hardworking, no-nonsense, probably drafts pitching earlier than most. Whether that translates to wins remains to be seen. The eighth pick can yield excellent value if managed correctly. The margin for error, however, is thin. *Projected finish: 9th*

**Pablo Sanchez's Drug Dealer** (Pick 9): One of the more memorable franchise names in league history, and one that demands respect for its commitment to the bit. The ninth pick begins the back tier of the draft, where value must be manufactured rather than handed over. There is no recent finish data to guide projections here, which makes this another wild card in an already unpredictable field. The name alone suggests a manager who does not take themselves too seriously—which can be either an asset or a liability in the pressure cooker of the fantasy baseball playoffs. *Projected finish: 11th*

**Hung Jung** (Pick 10): Drafting tenth is not a death sentence, but it does require a specific approach—an emphasis on identifying second-round value and refusing to reach in the first. Without historical data, this franchise remains a cipher. The name offers no clues. The draft position offers only challenges. We shall see what emerges. *Projected finish: 12th*

**Benchwarmers** (Pick 11): A fifth-place finish in 2021 followed by seventh in 2022 suggests a franchise that is quietly slipping. The trajectory is not dramatic—this is not a collapse—but it is concerning. Drafting eleventh makes the path back to contention even steeper. The **Benchwarmers** have shown they can compete in the top half of the league, but the momentum has shifted in the wrong direction. This is a pivotal season for the franchise's identity. *Projected finish: 7th*

**Buttville Skidmarks** (Pick 12): The commitment to this franchise name across multiple seasons is, in its own way, admirable. Drafting twelfth means this team will need to be creative, aggressive, and perhaps a bit lucky to climb into contention. There is no shame in a mid-pack finish from this draft position, but there is also no glory in it. The **Skidmarks** must decide what kind of season they want to have. *Projected finish: 13th*

**Riverside Roughnecks** (Pick 13): The thirteenth pick is unlucky by reputation and challenging by design. The **Roughnecks** will need to extract maximum value from every selection and hit on their late-round targets to have any chance at respectability. A new franchise name with no historical data means this is a blank slate—which is both terrifying and liberating. The Roughnecks could surprise everyone. They could also fade into irrelevance by the All-Star break. The variance at this draft position is simply enormous. *Projected finish: 14th*

**Hiroshima Radiation** (Pick 14): A third-place finish in 2022 from the franchise drafting fourteenth in 2023 is the single most fascinating storyline of this preview. The **Radiation** proved last year that draft position is not destiny—that smart management, aggressive trading, and waiver wire excellence can overcome a disadvantageous starting point. Drafting last is brutal, but this franchise has earned the benefit of the doubt. They know how to build a winner from the back of the draft. Do not sleep on them. *Projected finish: 5th*

**IV. Five Bold Predictions**

1. **The Abbotsford-Colby Twins will finish higher than the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs for the first time in two years.** The Twins have been steadily climbing while the Wonderdogs carry the psychological burden of last year's championship round loss. Third place and fourth place will flip in 2023.

2. **Hiroshima Radiation will reach the championship round despite drafting fourteenth.** They proved in 2022 that they can compete from any position. This is a franchise built on process, not draft capital, and they will prove it again.

3. **Sugar Land Skeeters will improve by at least six positions from their 2022 finish.** The second overall pick is a franchise-altering asset, and the Skeeters have been too bad for too long. Regression to the mean—upward—is coming.

4. **BaseballPro77 will finish outside the top four for the third consecutive season.** Despite holding the first overall pick, this franchise has shown a ceiling they cannot seem to break through. The pattern will hold.

5. **The Benchwarmers' two-year slide will continue, resulting in their worst finish since joining the league.** From fifth to seventh to... where? The trajectory is unmistakable, and drafting eleventh offers little hope for reversal.

**V. The Pick: A Champion Crowned**

The defending champions enter 2023 with a target on their back, a favorable draft position in the sweet spot of the snake, and a track record of excellence that demands respect. The **Albuquerque Bombers** have finished first and second in consecutive seasons. They understand roster construction. They know how to manage through injuries and slumps. They have proven they can handle the pressure of a championship chase and emerge victorious.

Other franchises will make noise. The **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** will compete. The **Hiroshima Radiation** will scheme from the back of the draft. But when October arrives and the championship is decided, it will be the **Bombers** raising the trophy once again. Dynasties are rare in fantasy baseball—the variance is too high, the luck too fickle. But this franchise has the look of something special. Back-to-back champions. Remember where you heard it first.
